properties.Title: Pergola automation motorization system design and cost optimization

text.content: Executive Summary

rich_text.plain_text: Executive Summary

text.content: Yannick seeks to motorize his climate pergola with home automation integration. Manus provides comprehensive motorization solutions, including cost analysis and technical specifications. When Yannick reveals he has 6 roofs to automate, Manus redesigns the solution to optimize costs through hybrid configurations, reducing expenses from 2,141€ to 1,004€ using a 3+3 motor setup with mechanical transmission.

markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Yannick seeks to motorize his climate pergola with home automation integration. Manus provides comprehensive motorization solutions, including cost analysis and technical specifications. When Yannick reveals he has 6 roofs to automate, Manus redesigns the solution to optimize costs through hybrid configurations, reducing expenses from 2,141€ to 1,004€ using a 3+3 motor setup with mechanical transmission.


## Context & Intent

User wants to motorize a climate pergola with adjustable slats using his DIY and programming skills, later revealing the scope includes 6 separate roof sections that need cost-effective automation


## What Was Done

Analyzed pergola motorization requirements, provided technical specifications for motors, designed cost-optimized solutions for multi-zone control, and compared different approaches including centralized vs distributed motor configurations


## Outputs Produced

- [technical_guide] Complete Pergola Motorization Guide — Comprehensive guide with motor specifications, installation procedures, and home automation integration
- [cost_analysis] Multi-Solution Cost Comparison — Detailed breakdown of 4 different motorization approaches with costs and trade-offs
- [recommendation] Hybrid 3+3 Motor Configuration — Optimized solution using 3 GM120 motors with mechanical transmission for 6 roofs

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Selected Telco GM120 motor as optimal solution for adjustable slat pergolas
- Recommended hybrid 3+3 configuration to reduce costs by 53% for 6-roof installation
- Prioritized mechanical transmission over centralized motor systems for reliability

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- Systematic analysis of motor requirements based on pergola specifications
- Cost optimization through creative mechanical solutions
- Comparative analysis of multiple vendor solutions
Failed / suboptimal:

- Initial single-roof assumption required complete redesign when scope expanded
- Credit limitation prevented website deployment completion
Discoveries:

- Mechanical transmission can reduce motorization costs by over 50% for multi-zone systems
- GM120 motors offer excellent price-performance ratio for DIY pergola automation

## Challenges & Blockers

- Budget constraints with 6 roofs requiring individual motorization
- Credit limitation preventing website deployment
- Balancing cost optimization with system reliability and maintenance complexity

## Open Questions

- Detailed installation procedures for hybrid 3+3 configuration
- Integration specifications with existing home automation board
- Long-term maintenance requirements for mechanical transmission systems

## Next Steps

- Provide detailed installation guide for recommended hybrid solution
- Specify home automation integration protocols and wiring
- Consider weather sensor integration for automatic operation
---
